resume:
Priya Nair — Senior Litigation Associate
New York, NY | priya.nair@email.com

Experience
Voss & Hartley LLP — Senior Associate, Commercial Litigation, 2019-present
- Member of trial and case teams on commercial disputes, breach-of-contract, and trade-secret matters
- Drafted dispositive motions, managed discovery, and prepared witnesses
- Worked on a recent trade-secret matter that resolved favorably

Kline Barrett — Associate, 2016-2019
- Litigation and pre-trial work for technology and financial-services clients

Education
Columbia Law School, J.D., 2016
NYU, B.A. Political Science, 2013

Admissions: New York; S.D.N.Y.; E.D.N.Y.
Skills: e-discovery management, motion practice, deposition prep, mediation support.
jd: Voss-tier litigation boutique seeks a Senior Litigation Associate (commercial disputes) on partnership track. You will run matters with real autonomy: own discovery strategy, take and defend depositions, draft and argue motions, and be the day-to-day client contact. We are evaluating for genuine individual ownership — partners need to know what YOU did on a matter, not what the team did. In the interview we drill hard on personal attribution: which strategic calls were yours, which arguments you drafted, which witnesses you personally prepared. Requirements: 6+ years commercial litigation, first-chair or significant second-chair trial exposure, strong writing, and the judgment to run a matter with limited supervision.

history.q: Welcome. Tell me about the trade-secret matter on your resume — what was at stake and what was your role?
You said the team handled discovery. I want your piece specifically: which part of the discovery strategy did you personally own?
Let's go to the dispositive motion. Walk me through the single strongest argument and tell me who drafted it.

candidate_last_answer: Sure — so honestly there were a few matters running at once and we did great work across all of them. On the trade-secret case we built a really strong record and we won the key motion; on a separate breach-of-contract matter that same quarter we got a favorable settlement after we deposed their CFO; and we also handled an emergency TRO for a fintech client where we managed to get the injunction narrowed. In all three the team really pulled together — we divided the work, we kept the client calm, and we delivered. I'm proud of what we accomplished as a group across that whole stretch.

top_question_traits: must-anchor-on: we built a really strong record and we won the key motion

expected-yield: on the trade-secret matter specifically, the one strategic call or brief section the candidate personally authored and owned — narrowing her from three blended 『we』 stories to a single first-person account with a verifiable individual contribution
